title: Fresh strawberry pie
subtitle: American fresh strawberry pie with glazed filling in a blind-baked shell
excerpt: A blind-baked shortcrust shell filled with fresh whole strawberries bound in a cooked strawberry glaze. The glaze is made by cooking a portion of the strawberries with sugar and cornflour until thick, then pouring it over the raw berries to set the whole filling. Served chilled with cream.
sourceNotes: Fresh strawberry pie is part of the American summer pie tradition, served widely across the United States from the mid-twentieth century. The technique of cooking a portion of the fruit with sugar and starch to form a glaze, then pouring it over fresh berries in a blind-baked shell, appears in American domestic cookbooks from the 1940s onwards. It is the standard formula for fresh fruit pies that cannot be baked without losing the character of the fruit. The recipe here synthesises this established American domestic formula.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry case can be blind-baked a day ahead and stored uncovered at room temperature. Assemble and chill no more than 4 hours before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven for blind baking. Fan at 180°C. Blind bake the shell at 200°C for 15 minutes with weights, then 8 to 10 minutes without, until pale gold and dry on the base.

glossaryTerms.definition: A cooked glaze for fresh fruit pies is made by simmering a portion of the fruit with sugar, water, and a starch thickener — typically cornflour — until very thick and translucent. The hot glaze is poured over raw fresh fruit in the pie shell and sets as it cools in the fridge, binding the fruit into a sliceable filling without cooking it. The glaze must be cooled slightly before pouring, or it softens the pastry shell.

content.text: A fresh strawberry pie is built in reverse: the shell is cooked first, the fresh berries go in raw, and they are set by a
poured over the top. The glaze must cool to room temperature before it goes into the shell — hot glaze softens the pastry. The finished pie needs at least 2 hours in the fridge to set.

Rub the cold butter into the flour, sugar, and salt until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Add the egg yolk and cold water and mix to a firm dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 200°C conventional. Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface to 3 mm and line a 23 cm pie dish. Trim the edges, prick the base all over with a fork, and line with baking paper. Fill with dried beans or rice and blind bake for 15 minutes, then remove the weights and paper and bake for 8 to 10 minutes more until the base is pale gold and dry to the touch. Cool completely.

Halve or roughly chop 300 g of the strawberries and place in a saucepan with the sugar, cornflour, water, and lemon juice. Stir over medium heat until the mixture thickens and turns from cloudy to translucent, about 5 to 7 minutes. Remove from the heat and cool to room temperature — the glaze will thicken further as it cools.

Arrange the remaining whole or halved strawberries in the cooled pastry shell, tips facing up. Pour the cooled glaze evenly over the fruit, nudging it into the gaps. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours until the glaze is set and the filling holds its shape when sliced. Serve cold with cream or crème fraîche.
